methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $205M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Jake Becraft and Tasuku Kitada, having raised $205M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—supplemented by 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $205M total raised proxy, implying a $390M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage private companies yields an $810M implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied owing to absence of revenue disclosure, though the sector shows a 5.8x median price-to-sales multiple across the three public tickers.

The triangulated range spans $331M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $600M. This range reflects material dispersion in peer valuations; the peer median of $810M sits in the upper half, while the stage-progression method anchors conservatively at $390M. Key uncertainties include Strand's position within the peer cohort on technology maturity and capital efficiency, and whether comparable companies' valuations reflect stage-appropriate risk adjustment.
